mission.title: Install and Configure Porter on Kubernetes
mission.description: Porter enables you to package your application artifacts, client tools, configuration, and deployment logic into a single installer that can be easily distributed and executed. Installing Porter allows you to simplify your application deployment process using the Cloud Native Application Bundle Specification.

steps.title: Add the Porter Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Porter Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add porter https://getporter.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Porter using Helm
steps.description:
Install Porter in your Kubernetes cluster using the following command. This will create a namespace called 'porter' if it doesn't already exist:
```bash
helm install porter porter/porter --version v1.4.0 --namespace porter --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Porter pods are running successfully with the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace porter
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for Porter
steps.description:
To ensure that Porter has appropriate resource limits, you can edit the deployment with the following command:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment porter --namespace porter
```
Add resource limits under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 512Mi
  requests:
    cpu: 250m
    memory: 256Mi
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Porter will show the Porter pod running in the 'porter' namespace. You can also verify that the resource limits have been applied correctly by checking the deployment configuration.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add porter https://getporter.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install porter porter/porter --version v1.4.0 --namespace porter --create-namespace

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 512Mi
  requests:
    cpu: 250m
    memory: 256Mi

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed on your local machine.
